title: Impact simulation should continuously scan CSS position properties for Amygdala cross-referencing

context.what_i_was_doing: Debugging a silent failure where the Hierarchy Popup didn't render on screen

observation: We spent unnecessary cycles hunting down why the Hierarchy Popup wasn't rendering. It was a `position: absolute` element receiving viewport-relative `getBoundingClientRect()` coordinates, causing it to render thousands of pixels offscreen mathematically. The Amygdala already had an exact matching warning for this logic flaw (`6f81ae96-1f6b-4e88-94b4-607f4398bc2c`), but it didn't trigger because the popup was a newly injected element without direct historical synapses to that specific error.
suggested_improvement: Plexus's impact simulation engine should parse basic frontend syntax. If a node in the blast radius introduces a new `position: absolute` or `position: fixed` CSS rule alongside `getBoundingClientRect()` JS execution, Plexus should automatically cross-reference historical Amygdala positioning bugs and flag the risk without needing a manual node-to-node Synapse bond.
expected_impact_on_plexus: Plexus transitions from a passive historical registry to an active static-analysis guardian, catching mathematical rendering bugs before code execution.
priority_reasoning: High — CSS coordinate mismatches are the #1 cause of silent visual failures in Nudge. Automating this check via semantic parsing prevents massive workflow friction.
